questions.text: How many pairs of ribs are there in a human body?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: 12**

Humans have **12 pairs (24 ribs)**.

Option Analysis:
• 10 → Incorrect.
• 11 → Incorrect.
• 12 → Correct.
• 13 → Incorrect.

Exam Tip: Ribs are classified into **true ribs (1–7), false ribs (8–10), floating ribs (11–12)**.

questions.text: Which one of the following salts is found in maximum amount in the human bones?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Calcium Phosphate**

Bones contain **hydroxyapatite crystals (calcium phosphate)** that provide strength.

questions.text: Which one of the following is the smallest bone in the human body?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Stapes**

The **stapes** is located in the middle ear and measures about **3 mm**.

questions.text: The smallest bone in our body is found in our
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Ear**

The stapes bone is part of the **ear ossicles (malleus, incus, stapes)**.

questions.text: The number of bones in human skull is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: 28**

The skull includes **cranial bones and facial bones**.

questions.text: Which part of the human body produces bile?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Liver**

The liver produces bile which helps in **fat digestion**.

questions.text: Which organ purifies blood in the human body?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Kidney**

Kidneys filter blood and remove nitrogenous waste.

questions.text: Which part of the brain controls balance of the body?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Cerebellum**

The cerebellum maintains **balance, posture and coordination**.

questions.text: Which vitamin helps in blood clotting?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Vitamin K**

Vitamin K helps synthesize **clotting factors like prothrombin**.

questions.text: Which gland secretes insulin?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Pancreas**

Insulin is secreted by **beta cells of Islets of Langerhans**.

questions.text: Which part of blood carries oxygen?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: RBC**

Red blood cells contain **haemoglobin**, which transports oxygen.

questions.text: Which organ pumps blood in the body?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Heart**

The heart pumps blood through **arteries and veins**.

questions.text: Which gas is mainly exhaled during respiration?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Carbon dioxide**

Respiration produces **CO₂ as a metabolic waste product**.

questions.text: Which organ is responsible for filtering lymph?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Spleen**

The spleen filters blood and stores immune cells.

questions.text: Which pigment gives red colour to blood?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Haemoglobin**

Haemoglobin binds oxygen in red blood cells.

questions.text: Which part of the digestive system absorbs most nutrients?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Small intestine**

The **villi and microvilli** increase absorption surface.

questions.text: Which part of the body regulates hormones?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Pituitary gland**

The pituitary gland is called the **master gland**.

questions.text: Which organ is responsible for hearing?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Ear**

The ear converts sound waves into nerve signals.

questions.text: Which component of blood helps in clotting?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Platelets**

Platelets initiate **blood coagulation**.

questions.text: Which organ stores bile produced by the liver?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Gall bladder**

The gall bladder stores and concentrates bile.
